name: New York Loan Company
description_short: New York Loan Company provides short-term collateral loans using luxury assets like watches, jewelry, fine art, and diamonds as security, with offices in Manhattan's Bryant Park.
description_long:
New York Loan Company is a collateral-based lender operating from Bryant Park in Manhattan, specializing in loans secured by high-value luxury assets. The company is part of Luxury Asset Capital, which it identifies as the nation's largest privately-held provider of non-bank loans using borrowers' luxury assets as collateral. The company has loaned over $1 billion to hundreds of thousands of clients according to their website.

New York Loan Company offers three primary loan products: Collateral Loans (short-term loans with specific capital amounts secured by luxury assets), Luxury Lines of Credit (revolving credit lines based on asset valuation), and Inventory Loans (for luxury goods retailers and wholesalers to use current inventory as collateral). They accept fine watches (Rolex, Cartier, Patek Philippe), high-end jewelry (Cartier, Harry Winston, Tiffany & Co.), fine art (contemporary and modern works), and certified diamonds and gemstones (GIA, Gubelin, AGL certified).

The company differentiates itself through several claimed advantages: their team includes certified gemologists and experts across multiple luxury asset classes, they provide funding in minutes with "no time-consuming paperwork," the loans are not reported to credit bureaus, and they maintain private offices in Manhattan. They position themselves as an alternative to traditional financing channels and asset sales, emphasizing speed and discretion.

Prospective borrowers should note that while New York Loan Company operates legally in the collateral lending space, the lack of credit bureau reporting means the loan won't help build credit history. The company's website does not prominently display interest rates, fees, or specific loan terms, which is typical for asset-based lenders but limits transparency. Borrowers should conduct thorough due diligence on rates and terms before engaging.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Funding available in minutes with minimal paperwork according to company claims
Loan activity not reported to credit bureaus, maintaining borrower privacy
Team includes certified gemologists and luxury asset experts per website
Accepts wide range of high-value assets including watches, jewelry, fine art, and certified gemstones
Offers flexible revolving line of credit option in addition to standard collateral loans
Specifically serves luxury goods retailers and traders with inventory loan products
Established track record with over $1 billion loaned to hundreds of thousands of clients
cons: Interest rates, APR, and specific fee structures not disclosed on website, making cost comparison difficult
Loan does not report to credit bureaus, so borrowing activity cannot help build credit history
Requires physical collateral with significant liquidation value, limiting access for those without luxury assets
Website states office closed early on Fridays and weekends by appointment only, limiting accessibility
No information provided about what happens to assets if loan defaults or borrower cannot repay
best_for: High-net-worth individuals with luxury assets who need quick capital without traditional loan approval processes
Owners of fine watches, jewelry, art, or certified gemstones who require short-term financing
Luxury goods retailers and wholesalers needing working capital without inventory liquidation
Borrowers seeking confidential financing that won't appear on credit reports
diagnosis: New York Loan Company is best suited for affluent individuals or luxury goods businesses needing rapid capital against high-value collateral, particularly those prioritizing discretion and speed over credit-building. The primary caveat is that undisclosed rates and fees make it essential to request complete pricing terms before committing, and borrowers should understand that this financing model does not help establish or improve credit history.
services: Short-term collateral loans secured by luxury assets
Revolving luxury lines of credit based on asset valuation
Inventory loans for luxury goods trade members and retailers
Fine watch lending (Rolex, Cartier, Audemars Piguet, Patek Philippe)
High-end jewelry loans (Cartier, David Webb, Graff, Harry Winston, Tiffany & Co.)
Fine art lending (contemporary and modern works)
Certified diamond and gemstone lending (GIA, Gubelin, AGL certified)
In-person appointments at Bryant Park Manhattan office
Asset valuation by certified gemologists and luxury experts
Secure asset storage during loan term
Same-day or rapid funding via cash or wire transfer
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
address: 110 W 40th St Suite 605, New York, NY 10018
